title: Lesson 209: (Grammar) - to be bad at doing something

intro_cards.title: Being Bad At: のが下手
intro_cards.body:
のが下手 (へた) means 'to be bad at doing ~' / 'not good at ~.'

Pattern: [plain verb]のが下手です

• 私は泳ぐのが下手です。(I'm bad at swimming.)
• 嘘をつくのが下手な人。(A person who is bad at lying.)

Note: 下手 can sound blunt about others.
For tact, use とくいじゃない or にがて instead.

Related:
• 上手 (じょうず) — good at (praising others)
• とくい — one's strong point
• にがて — weak at / uncomfortable with

Saying 日本語が下手 about yourself is humble; about others it can be rude.
